title: Rudbeckia auriculata
taxonomy: Rudbeckia auriculata is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the order Asterales. The genus Rudbeckia is further categorized within the tribe Heliantheae, which includes other plants such as coneflowers and black-eyed Susans. At the higher taxonomic level, Rudbeckia auriculata is a part of the clade that includes the angiosperms, or flowering plants. The combination of its family, genus, and species classification makes Rudbeckia auriculata a distinct and recognized member of the plant kingdom.
uses: Rudbeckia auriculata has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and pain. Additionally, some studies suggest that Rudbeckia auriculata may have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects. More studies are necessary to fully understand the benefits and risks of using Rudbeckia auriculata for medicinal purposes.
